comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The solution writes the finite free convolution as

  p(x) ⊞_n q(x) = Σ_{π∈S_n} Π_{i=1}^n (x − α_i − β_{π(i)})

The right-hand side has leading coefficient n! (one contribution per permutation), so it is NOT monic. This contradicts the problem statement, which defines p⊞_nq as a monic polynomial with c_0=1.

The correct formula (consistent with the Marcus–Spielman–Srivastava coefficient definition c_k = Σ_{i+j=k} (n−i)!(n−j)!/(n!(n−k)!) a_i b_j) is:

  p(x) ⊞_n q(x) = (1/n!) Σ_{π∈S_n} Π_{i=1}^n (x − α_i − β_{π(i)})

For the purposes of the proof, the roots (and hence Φ_n, hyperbolicity, and the Jacobian) are unchanged by the scalar factor n!, so no downstream step is invalidated. However, the formula as stated is mathematically incorrect and conflicts with the monic normalisation used throughout the proof and problem statement.

comments.author: system
comments.body:
**Proof file:** `webapp/issues/q4/working_solution.tex`

The issue described above occurs in this working solution file. Reviewers should consult the file at that path for the exact context.
